mappings.rationaleAI: Ecological principles address biosphere organization (A.2), energy flow (A.3), ecosystem definition (B.1), biotic components (B.2), and ecosystem productivity (B.6).
Integrated Earth system addresses biosphere preconditions (A.1), energy flow (A.3), carbon cycling (A.4.b), and the noosphere through sustainability (A.5).
Atmospheric composition and greenhouse effect address carbon cycles (A.4.b) and radiation as abiotic component (B.3.b).
Climate dynamics address carbon cycles (A.4.b) and pressure/temperature abiotic components (B.3.a).
Water properties address the water cycle (A.4.e) and water as abiotic component (B.3.d).
Nitrogen fixation addresses the nitrogen cycle (A.4.c), and decomposers serve as biotic components (B.2.c).
Forest carbon cycling addresses carbon cycles (A.4.b) and ecosystem structure and productivity (B.1, B.2, B.6).
Ocean biogeochemical cycling addresses carbon (A.4.b) and nitrogen cycles (A.4.c), and water characteristics (B.3.d).
Marine diversity and biogeochemistry address energy flow (A.3), biotic components (B.2), and ecosystem productivity (B.6).
Geographic distribution addresses biosphere organization (A.2) and ecosystem concepts (B).
Fire ecology addresses fire as a limiting factor (B.3.f).
Essential mineral nutrients address sedimentary cycles (A.4.f).
Human environmental impact addresses mankind's place in the biosphere (A.5) and abiotic conditioning by organisms (B.4).
Life's origins and habitability conditions address biosphere preconditions (A.1).
